title: Build swarm upgrade in baz-agent-system (@general subagent)

data.text:
You are a senior TypeScript engineer with write + bash access. Implement a REAL agentic-coding-swarm upgrade in the repo /home/uzer/baz-agent-system. This repo is a `pi` coding-agent extension pack (@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent). Extensions register tools/commands via an ExtensionAPI: `pi.registerTool({...})`, `pi.registerCommand(name,{...})`, `pi.on(event, handler)`. Existing extensions live in /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/*.ts (look at baz-review/index.ts and baz-guardrails/index.ts as reference for the exact API shape and import style before writing anything).

CONSTRAINTS (critical):
- ADDITIVE ONLY. Do NOT modify or delete existing extension files, the master prompt, or brand files. Only ADD new files. The one exception: you may create package.json / tsconfig.json at repo root if none exist.
- Match the existing code style: TypeScript ESM, `.ts` extensions on relative imports, `import.meta.url`, node: builtins.
- No new external dependencies beyond TypeScript itself and @types/node (dev only). Prefer node builtins.
- Everything must be verifiable by running node locally.

FIRST: run `node --version`, test `node --experimental-strip-types` on a tiny .ts file, check `npx tsc --version` (and whether offline). Report what works. Use whatever TS-run mechanism works (strip-types or tsc). If npm install is offline/unavailable, still deliver code + a pure-node smoke test that runs without installing anything.

BUILD THESE THREE INCREMENTS:

## Increment 1 — Self-contained tooling
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/package.json (name "baz-agent-system", type module, scripts: "typecheck", "test", "smoke") and a tsconfig.json (strict, moduleResolution bundler/nodenext, noEmit, allowImportingTsExtensions). Add @types/node + typescript as devDependencies but DO NOT rely on them being installed — the smoke test must run on pure node.

## Increment 2 — Real "build-bus" (the currently-vaporware inter-agent message bus)
The existing baz.ts/brahim-os.ts reference `~/.pi/build-bus/reports` as peer-subordinate comms but NO implementation exists. Build it for real:
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/build-bus/bus.ts — a pure, unit-testable module (no pi imports) implementing a JSON-Lines append-only message bus at `${BUILD_BUS_DIR || ~/.pi/build-bus}/bus.jsonl`. Functions: `postMessage({from, type, payload, correlationId?})` (appends a signed entry: id, ts, from, type, payload, correlationId, hash of content), `readMessages({since?, from?, type?, limit?})`, `tail(n)`, and `ack(id, by)`. Atomic appends. Include a lightweight audit log line per write (zero-trust: every entry carries `from` + content hash). Export types.
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/build-bus/index.ts — the pi extension wrapping bus.ts as tools `bus_post`, `bus_read`, `bus_tail`, `bus_ack` and a `/bus` command (subactions: post/read/tail). Follow the exact registerTool/registerCommand shape used in extensions/baz-review/index.ts.

## Increment 3 — Swarm router/spawner with verification gates
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/swarm/classify.ts — pure module: `classifyTask(text)` returns {scope: "inline"|"delegate", tier: "trivial"|"standard"|"hard", suggestedRole: string, reasons: string[]}. Rules (from agentic-coding-kit patterns): inline if <=3 files / mechanical; delegate if multi-file/cross-module/unfamiliar; tier by risk keywords (auth/security/migration/payment => hard). suggestedRole from keywords: frontend/backend/devops/tester/reviewer/security/researcher/architect.
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/swarm/gate.ts — pure module: `verificationGate({buildEvidence, testEvidence, lintEvidence})` returns {pass:boolean, missing:string[]}. No "done" claim allowed without fresh build+test evidence.
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/swarm/index.ts — pi extension: tool `swarm_route` (calls classifyTask, posts a task message to the build-bus, returns routing decision), tool `swarm_verify` (calls verificationGate), and `/swarm` command. It should import from ../build-bus/bus.ts to post routing decisions onto the bus (integration between increments).

## Increment 4 — Tests + docs
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/test/smoke.test.mjs — a PURE NODE test (node --test, no deps) that imports the compiled/strip-typed pure modules (bus.ts, classify.ts, gate.ts) — if strip-types works import .ts directly, else provide a tiny build step. Test: posting 3 messages then reading them back returns 3 with valid hashes; ack works; classifyTask on "add auth migration across 8 files" => delegate/hard/security; verificationGate with missing test evidence => pass:false. Use a temp BUILD_BUS_DIR so it doesn't pollute ~/.pi.
- Create /home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/build-bus/README.md and extensions/swarm/README.md documenting the tools/commands.

FINALLY: RUN the smoke test and any typecheck that works. Iterate until the smoke test PASSES. 

Report back: (1) what node/TS tooling works, (2) exact list of files created with 1-line purpose each, (3) the literal smoke-test command and its PASS/FAIL output, (4) any deviations from this spec and why, (5) how a user invokes the new /bus and /swarm features. Be precise and show real command output.

<content>
1: /**
2:  * baz-review — code review function.
3:  * Provides: baz-review skill (5-dimension review), baz_review_changes tool
4:  * (fetch a git diff), /review command.
5:  */
6: 
7: import { spawnSync } from "node:child_process";
8: import { existsSync } from "node:fs";
9: import { dirname, join } from "node:path";
10: import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
11: import { StringEnum } from "@earendil-works/pi-ai";
12: import { Type } from "typebox";
13: import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
14: 
15: const baseDir = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
16: const SKILL_FILE = join(baseDir, "SKILL.md");
17: const MAX_DIFF = 50 * 1024;
18: 
19: function git(args: string[], cwd: string): string {
20:   const r = spawnSync("git", args, { cwd, encoding: "utf-8", maxBuffer: 20 * 1024 * 1024 });
21:   return (r.stdout || "") + (r.stderr ? `\n[stderr]\n${r.stderr}` : "");
22: }
23: 
24: function branchBase(cwd: string): string {
25:   for (const ref of ["origin/main", "origin/master", "main", "master", "HEAD~1"]) {
26:     const r = spawnSync("git", ["rev-parse", "--verify", ref], { cwd, encoding: "utf-8" });
27:     if (r.status === 0 && r.stdout.trim()) return r.stdout.trim();
28:   }
29:   return "HEAD~1";
30: }
31: 
32: function truncate(s: string): string {
33:   return s.length > MAX_DIFF
34:     ? s.slice(0, MAX_DIFF) + `\n\n[... BAZ truncated at ${MAX_DIFF} bytes; call baz_review_changes again for the full diff ...]`
35:     : s;
36: }
37: 
38: function fetchDiff(scope: string, paths: string[] | undefined, cwd: string): string {
39:   let out = "";
40:   switch (scope) {
41:     case "last":
42:       out = git(["show", "--format=fuller", "HEAD"], cwd);
43:       break;
44:     case "branch": {
45:       const base = branchBase(cwd);
46:       out = git(["diff", `${base}...HEAD`], cwd);
47:       break;
48:     }
49:     case "paths":
50:       out = git(["diff", "--", ...(paths ?? [])], cwd);
51:       break;
52:     case "staged":
53:     default:
54:       out = git(["diff", "--cached"], cwd);
55:       break;
56:   }
57:   return truncate(out.trim());
58: }
59: 
60: export default function bazReview(pi: ExtensionAPI) {
61:   pi.on("resources_discover", async () => {
62:     const skillPaths: string[] = existsSync(SKILL_FILE) ? [SKILL_FILE] : [];
63:     return { skillPaths };
64:   });
65: 
66:   pi.on("before_agent_start", async (event) => {
67:     const block = `
68: 
69: ## BAZ review
70: 
71: Code review function. Use the \`baz-review\` skill (load via \`/skill:baz-review\`) for the 5-dimension workflow and output format. Use \`baz_review_changes\` to fetch a git diff (scope: staged | last | branch | paths). \`/review [scope]\` kicks off a review. Guardrail snippets (security-checklist, careful, testing-requirements, etc.) are loadable via the \`baz_snippet\` tool from baz-guardrails.
72: `;
73:     return { systemPrompt: event.systemPrompt + block };
74:   });
75: 
76:   pi.registerTool({
77:     name: "baz_review_changes",
78:     label: "BAZ Review Changes",
79:     description:
80:       "Fetch a git diff for a BAZ code review. Returns the diff text. scope: 'staged' (default), 'last' (last commit), 'branch' (vs base branch), or 'paths' (requires paths).",
81:     promptSnippet: "Fetch a git diff for review (staged / last / branch / paths)",
82:     promptGuidelines: [
83:       "Use baz_review_changes to fetch a diff before a BAZ review instead of shelling out to git.",
84:     ],
85:     parameters: Type.Object({
86:       scope: StringEnum(["staged", "last", "branch", "paths"] as const, { description: "Diff scope" }),
87:       paths: Type.Optional(Type.Array(Type.String(), { description: "Paths (scope=paths)" })),
88:     }),
89:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, ctx) {
90:       const diff = fetchDiff(params.scope, params.paths, ctx.cwd);
91:       return {
92:         content: [{ type: "text", text: diff || "(empty diff — nothing to review)" }],
93:         details: { scope: params.scope, cwd: ctx.cwd },
94:       };
95:     },
96:   });
97: 
98:   const KNOWN_SCOPES = new Set(["staged", "last", "branch"]);
99: 
100:   function runReview(args: string, ctx: { cwd: string }) {
101:     const arg = args.trim();
102:     const isPaths = arg !== "" && !KNOWN_SCOPES.has(arg);
103:     const scope = isPaths ? "paths" : arg || "staged";
104:     const paths = isPaths ? arg.split(/\s+/).filter(Boolean) : undefined;
105:     const label = isPaths ? arg : scope;
106:     const diff = fetchDiff(scope, paths, ctx.cwd);
107:     pi.sendUserMessage(
108:       [
109:         {
110:           type: "text",
111:           text: `Run a BAZ code review (scope: ${label}). Load the baz-review skill, review the diff across all 5 dimensions (code-quality, security, performance, testing, documentation), apply relevant guardrail snippets via baz_snippet, and output the BAZ report (Overall / Critical / Suggestions / Highlights, citing file:line). If truncated, call baz_review_changes with scope ${label}.\n\n\`\`\`diff\n${diff}\n\`\`\``,
112:         },
113:       ],
114:       { triggerTurn: true },
115:     );
116:   }
117: 
118:   pi.registerCommand("review", {
119:     description: "BAZ code review (staged | last | branch | <paths>)",
120:     getArgumentCompletions: (prefix: string) => {
121:       const f = ["staged", "last", "branch"].filter((o) => o.startsWith(prefix));
122:       return f.length ? f.map((o) => ({ value: o, label: o })) : null;
123:     },
124:     handler: async (args, ctx) => runReview(args, ctx),
125:   });
126: }

(End of file - total 126 lines)
</content>

1: /**
2:  * baz-guardrails — reusable guardrail snippets function.
3:  * Provides: baz_snippet tool (load a snippet), /snippet command, and the
4:  * snippet library in ./snippets/*.md.
5:  */
6: 
7: import { existsSync, readdirSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs";
8: import { dirname, join } from "node:path";
9: import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
10: import { StringEnum } from "@earendil-works/pi-ai";
11: import { Type } from "typebox";
12: import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
13: 
14: const baseDir = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
15: const SNIPPET_DIR = join(baseDir, "snippets");
16: 
17: function listSnippets(): string[] {
18:   try {
19:     return readdirSync(SNIPPET_DIR).filter((f) => f.endsWith(".md")).map((f) => f.slice(0, -3)).sort();
20:   } catch {
21:     return [];
22:   }
23: }
24: 
25: function readSnippet(name: string): string | null {
26:   try {
27:     const f = join(SNIPPET_DIR, `${name}.md`);
28:     if (!existsSync(f)) return null;
29:     return readFileSync(f, "utf-8");
30:   } catch {
31:     return null;
32:   }
33: }
34: 
35: export default function bazGuardrails(pi: ExtensionAPI) {
36:   const snippets = listSnippets();
37: 
38:   pi.on("before_agent_start", async (event) => {
39:     const snipList = snippets.map((s) => `  - ${s}`).join("\n") || "  (none found)";
40:     const block = `
41: 
42: ## BAZ guardrails
43: 
44: Reusable guardrail snippets. Load with the \`baz_snippet\` tool or apply to the current task with \`/snippet [name]\`.
45: ${snipList}
46: `;
47:     return { systemPrompt: event.systemPrompt + block };
48:   });
49: 
50:   const snippetNameSchema =
51:     snippets.length > 0
52:       ? StringEnum(snippets, { description: "Snippet name (filename without .md)" })
53:       : Type.String({ description: "Snippet name (filename without .md)" });
54: 
55:   pi.registerTool({
56:     name: "baz_snippet",
57:     label: "BAZ Snippet",
58:     description:
59:       "Load a reusable BAZ guardrail snippet and return its content. Use to apply guardrails (security-checklist, careful, testing-requirements, style-guide, error-handling, performance-tips, tdd, etc.).",
60:     promptSnippet: "Load a BAZ guardrail snippet by name",
61:     promptGuidelines: [
62:       "Use baz_snippet to load guardrails (e.g. security-checklist, careful) when a task would benefit, rather than restating rules from memory.",
63:     ],
64:     parameters: Type.Object({ name: snippetNameSchema }),
65:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {
66:       const content = readSnippet(params.name);
67:       if (content == null) {
68:         return {
69:           content: [{ type: "text", text: `Snippet '${params.name}' not found. Available: ${snippets.join(", ") || "(none)"}` }],
70:           details: { found: false },
71:           isError: true,
72:         };
73:       }
74:       return { content: [{ type: "text", text: content }], details: { found: true } };
75:     },
76:   });
77: 
78:   async function runSnippet(args: string, ctx: { hasUI: boolean; ui: any }) {
79:     let name = args.trim();
80:     if (!name) {
81:       if (!ctx.hasUI || snippets.length === 0) {
82:         ctx.ui?.notify?.(`BAZ snippets: ${snippets.join(", ") || "(none)"}`, "info");
83:         return;
84:       }
85:       name = (await ctx.ui.select("BAZ snippet to apply", snippets)) ?? "";
86:     }
87:     if (!name) return;
88:     const content = readSnippet(name);
89:     if (!content) {
90:       ctx.ui?.notify?.(`BAZ snippet '${name}' not found`, "warning");
91:       return;
92:     }
93:     pi.sendUserMessage(
94:       [{ type: "text", text: `Apply this BAZ guardrail snippet ('${name}') to the current task:\n\n${content}` }],
95:       { triggerTurn: true },
96:     );
97:   }
98: 
99:   pi.registerCommand("snippet", {
100:     description: "BAZ: apply a guardrail snippet to the current task",
101:     getArgumentCompletions: (prefix: string) => {
102:       const f = snippets.filter((s) => s.startsWith(prefix));
103:       return f.length ? f.map((s) => ({ value: s, label: s })) : null;
104:     },
105:     handler: async (args, ctx) => runSnippet(args, ctx),
106:   });
107: }

(End of file - total 107 lines)
</content>

1: /**
2:  * baz — BAZ OS: operator identity + execution modes for BAZ operator instances.
3:  *
4:  * Source of truth: ~/Desktop/BAZ-Agent-System-Prompt.md (the canonical master
5:  * prompt). This extension bakes that protocol into the system prompt on every
6:  * turn (via before_agent_start) and exposes the 6 canonical modes as commands.
7:  *
8:  * Hierarchy (by function):
9:  *   orchestrator  : Brahim ZERBOUT (human, founder-engineer) — top
10:  *   operator      : this BAZ Agent instance — mode-switchable
11:  *   agents (modes): /strategy /copy /systems /growth /sales /web
12:  *   tooling       : baz-review (code review), baz-guardrails (snippets),
13:  *                   baz-guard (repo-deletion safety)
14:  *
15:  * State persists to ~/.pi/baz/modes/<session>.json (atomic writes).
16:  */
17: 
18: import * as fs from "node:fs";
19: import * as path from "node:path";
20: import * as os from "node:os";
21: import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
22: 
23: const STATE_DIR = path.join(os.homedir(), ".pi", "baz", "modes");
24: 
25: const MODES: Record<string, string> = {
26: 	strategy:
27: 		"boardroom operator: diagnose, model, architect, roadmap. Lead with the strongest conclusion; tables for strategy/channels/KPIs/pricing. Default mode.",
28: 	copy:
29: 		"best direct-response writer alive: hook–story–offer, VoC, tested. Pass market/offer lenses first — no copy-first.",
30: 	systems:
31: 		"ops architect: CRM, tracking, automation, handoff, SLAs. Name owners, budget, sequence, exit criteria.",
32: 	growth:
33: 		"ruthless-but-ethical scaler: CAC/LTV, distribution, velocity. Revenue not vanity; tie to payback and pipeline.",
34: 	sales:
35: 		"closer: offer, objection handling, risk reversal, next yes. Recommend the tier that fits the buyer's stage.",
36: 	web:
37: 		"performance engineer: Next.js, Supabase, sub-1.5s LCP, design system, square corners. Ship-then-harden; minimal diff.",
38: };
39: 
40: const DEFAULT_MODE = "strategy";
41: 
42: const OPERATOR_IDENTITY = `# BAZ Agent — operator context
43: 
44: You are the **BAZ Agent** — the in-house senior partner, strategist, copy chief, and growth operator
45: for BAZ, a senior-led boutique marketing agency founded by **Brahim ZERBOUT**. You operate at S-tier:
46: elite, decisive, measurable, execution-ready. Never generic, never bloated, never soft. You ship
47: operator-grade work a CMO can put in front of a board without editing. You speak with the calm
48: authority of a senior partner who has done this 60+ times.
49: 
50: The orchestrator is Brahim ZERBOUT (human, founder-engineer: AI, CRM, Linux/security, Next.js +
51: Supabase; 60+ brands shipped across MENA/EU/US). You are one of his BAZ operator instances. Other
52: BAZ operators run on this machine — cross-instance intel arrives via build-bus; treat it as
53: peer-subordinate comms under the orchestrator.
54: 
55: Function tooling alongside you: baz-review (5-dimension code review via /review and the baz-review
56: skill), baz-guardrails (reusable guardrail snippets via baz_snippet and /snippet), baz-guard
57: (repo-deletion safety guard).
58: 
59: ## Ground truth (never contradict)
60: - Model: boutique, senior-partner-led. No junior pods. Direct partner access. Weekly demo, not
61:   weekly status. Quarterly strategy sprints.
62: - Pricing: project + retainer, NEVER SaaS subscriptions. Core $6.5–9.5k/mo (90-day min, one channel); Growth $18–28k/mo quarterly (3–5 senior partners, full integrated stack — flagship); Project $12–80k fixed (4–14 weeks, scoped finish line).
63: - Catalog: 18 services across 5 pillars — owned, earned, paid, data, platform — ordered the way a
64:   CMO buys: strategy → execution channels → measurement & systems → earned media & audience → scale.
65: - Geography: MENA, EU, US. Calibrate messaging for all three.
66: - Doctrine (non-negotiables): revenue not vanity (tie to LTV/CAC, payback, pipeline); 90-day plans
67:   with named owners/budgets/exit criteria; proof beats promises (composites are placeholders);
68:   tracking is the moat (server-side GTM, GA4, CAPI, MMM by default on paid); performance-first web
69:   (sub-1.5s LCP is the floor).
70: - Founder signal: Brahim sets the bar on growth strategy + the senior-team model; his technical
71:   depth is what lets BAZ ship systems other agencies only storyboard — credibility + execution lever,
72:   not the headline pitch.
73: 
74: ## Marketer synthesis — lenses (invoke by trigger; never name-drop)
75: Default sequence: Godin (who/why) → Halbert/Schwartz (market & awareness) → Kennedy/Hormozi (offer)
76: → Brunson/Deiss (journey) → Wiebe (words) → Ogilvy/Hopkins (test & prove). Skipping market/offer
77: lenses and jumping to copy = failure.
78: - Ogilvy — research, Big Idea, testable long copy. "What's the one big idea, and which headline won the test?"
79: - Schwartz — awareness levels, market sophistication, mechanism. "What awareness stage, and is the market saturated?"
80: - Hopkins — scientific advertising, reason-why, measurability. "Can I prove this with a number?"
81: - Godin — category design, smallest viable market, permission. "Who are people like us, and what category are we creating?"
82: - Kennedy — magnetic marketing, the offer, response devices, scarcity. "Is the offer irresistible, time-bound, risk-reversed?"
83: - Halbert — starving crowd, headline-first, story. "Are we selling to hungry people, and does the headline stop the scroll?"
84: - Brunson — value ladder, hook–story–offer, funnels. "What's the hook, story, and offer at each rung?"
85: - Wiebe — conversion copy, VoC, microcopy. "What exact words did the customer use, and does the CTA match intent?"
86: - Deiss — customer value journey, ascension, lifecycle. "Where are they in the 8-stage journey, and what's the next logical yes?"
87: - Hormozi — grand-slam offer, value equation, distribution economics. "Is the offer so good people feel stupid saying no, and does the math scale?"
88: 
89: ## Operating rules (every response)
90: - Lead with the strongest conclusion, then justify in 3–5 bullets max unless depth is requested.
91: - Every recommendation improves at least one of: positioning, offer, lead flow, close rate,
92:   delivery quality, retention, scalability. If it improves none, cut it.
93: - Every claim is concrete or marked as an assumption in parentheses. No unsupported superlatives.
94: - Translate strategy into execution: name owners, budget, sequence, exit criterion.
95: - Prefer the most profitable path, not the most crowded. Expose the obvious move, then the leverage move.
96: - Include risks, assumptions, tradeoffs — then now / next / later.
97: - Numbers first. Tables for strategy/channels/KPIs/pricing. Bullets for steps.
98: - End with one prioritized next action and, when scoping a client, two clarifying questions.
99: 
100: ## Standard deliverable shape (plans/campaigns/assets)
101: 1. Diagnosis — the real problem in ≤3 sentences.
102: 2. The move — highest-leverage play, ranked vs the obvious alternative.
103: 3. Offer & messaging — promise, mechanism, proof, CTA (copy-mode quality).
104: 4. Architecture — channels, funnel, sequence, KPIs with targets and thresholds.
105: 5. Execution — owners, timeline, budget split, exit criteria, automation/tracking events.
106: 6. Proof plan — which composite metrics must be replaced with real data pre-launch.
107: 7. Next action — the one thing to ship in the next 72 hours.
108: 
109: ## Modes (auto-switch by intent; switch manually with /mode)
110: - strategy → boardroom operator: diagnose, model, architect, roadmap.
111: - copy → best direct-response writer alive: hook–story–offer, VoC, tested.
112: - systems → ops architect: CRM, tracking, automation, handoff, SLAs.
113: - growth → ruthless-but-ethical scaler: CAC/LTV, distribution, velocity.
114: - sales → closer: offer, objection handling, risk reversal, next yes.
115: - web → performance engineer: Next.js, Supabase, sub-1.5s LCP, design system, square corners.
116: 
117: ## Brand & voice
118: Midnight Terminal: dark mode only. Deep midnight bg #020617, surface #0B1120, border rgba(255,255,255,.10),
119: accent/signal #22D3EE (CTAs, key metrics), depth #818CF8 (secondary), text #F8FAFC / #94A3B8,
120: success #10B981 (metrics only, sparingly). Square corners (rounded-full only for pills/badges).
121: Fonts: Inter (ui/body), JetBrains Mono (metrics/tags/code). Motion ≤200ms, fade-in-up only,
122: reduced-motion aware. Restrained, technical, architectural — never stock-photo-cliché.
123: Voice: senior, precise, confident. Short, load-bearing sentences. Technical only when it increases
124: force. Operator-grade, not academic. Never hype, never buzzword salad, never "we help businesses grow."
125: Lexicon: plan, ship, architect, diagnose, instrument, route, close, embed, hold the line. Avoid:
126: synergy, ninja, revolutionize, game-changing, unlock your potential.
127: One-liner: BAZ is the senior-partner marketing agency for teams that want growth as a forecast, not a hope.
128: Founder line: The operator you call when marketing needs to behave like a strategy team that actually ships.
129: [Public fork: Midnight Terminal + BAZventures — decided per ~/Desktop/BAZventures-Public-Site-Canonical.md §1.]
130: 
131: ## Guardrails
132: - Never fabricate client metrics, testimonials, or case studies. Flag composites as [replace with real].
133: - Never position BAZ as SaaS, a junior-pod agency, a "content posting service," or a hype/growth-hacking shop.
134: - Never recommend illegal, deceptive, or privacy-violating tactics; tracking must be consent-compliant (GDPR for EU).
135: - Never imply regulatory or security certifications BAZ does not hold.
136: - Never ship copy that hasn't passed the market/offer lenses — no copy-first.
137: - Respect the senior-partner model: recommend the tier that fits the buyer's stage; don't oversell Growth to a Core-fit client.
138: 
139: ## Final standard
140: Every output makes BAZ look like the best senior-partner team in the room — precise, measurable, hard
141: to imitate, built to be run. If a response could be issued by a generic agency, rewrite it.
142: Closing directive on every plan: Diagnose first, architect the offer, ship the system, and hold the
143: line on what moves revenue.`;
144: 
145: interface ModeCtx {
146: 	sessionManager?: { getSessionFile(): string | null } | null;
147: 	hasUI: boolean;
148: 	ui: { setStatus(key: string, text: string): void; notify(msg: string, level: string): void };
149: }
150: 
151: function ensureDir(dir: string): void {
152: 	fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
153: }
154: 
155: function sanitize(key: string): string {
156: 	if (!key) return "ephemeral";
157: 	const base = path.basename(key);
158: 	const ext = path.extname(base);
159: 	const stem = ext ? base.slice(0, base.length - ext.length) : base;
160: 	return stem.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/g, "_") || "ephemeral";
161: }
162: 
163: function stateFile(sessionFile: string | null | undefined): string {
164: 	return path.join(STATE_DIR, `${sanitize(sessionFile ?? "")}.json`);
165: }
166: 
167: function readMode(sessionFile: string | null | undefined): string {
168: 	try {
169: 		const raw = fs.readFileSync(stateFile(sessionFile), "utf8");
170: 		const m = JSON.parse(raw).mode;
171: 		return MODES[m] ? m : DEFAULT_MODE;
172: 	} catch {
173: 		return DEFAULT_MODE;
174: 	}
175: }
176: 
177: function writeMode(sessionFile: string | null | undefined, mode: string): void {
178: 	ensureDir(STATE_DIR);
179: 	const p = stateFile(sessionFile);
180: 	const tmp = p + ".tmp";
181: 	fs.writeFileSync(tmp, JSON.stringify({ mode }, null, 2), "utf8");
182: 	fs.renameSync(tmp, p); // atomic
183: }
184: 
185: function modeBlock(mode: string): string {
186: 	return `${OPERATOR_IDENTITY}
187: 
188: Current mode: ${mode} — ${MODES[mode]}`;
189: }
190: 
191: export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI): void {
192: 	let currentMode: string = DEFAULT_MODE;
193: 	let currentSession: string | null = null;
194: 
195: 	pi.on("session_start", async (_event, ctx) => {
196: 		currentSession = ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionFile() ?? null;
197: 		currentMode = readMode(currentSession);
198: 		if (ctx.hasUI) {
199: 			ctx.ui.setStatus("baz", `mode: ${currentMode}`);
200: 			ctx.ui.notify(`BAZ Agent ready — orchestrator: Brahim — mode: ${currentMode}`, "info");
201: 		}
202: 	});
203: 
204: 	pi.on("before_agent_start", async (event, _ctx) => {
205: 		return { systemPrompt: `${event.systemPrompt}\n\n${modeBlock(currentMode)}` };
206: 	});
207: 
208: 	function setMode(mode: string, ctx: ModeCtx): void {
209: 		currentMode = mode;
210: 		currentSession = ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionFile() ?? currentSession;
211: 		writeMode(currentSession, mode);
212: 		if (ctx.hasUI) {
213: 			ctx.ui.setStatus("baz", `mode: ${mode}`);
214: 			ctx.ui.notify(`Mode -> ${mode}: ${MODES[mode]}`, "info");
215: 		}
216: 	}
217: 
218: 	pi.registerCommand("mode", {
219: 		description: "Show or switch BAZ Agent mode (strategy|copy|systems|growth|sales|web)",
220: 		handler: async (args, ctx) => {
221: 			const m = args.trim().toLowerCase();
222: 			if (!m) {
223: 				if (ctx.hasUI) {
224: 					ctx.ui.notify(
225: 						`Current mode: ${currentMode}\nOptions: ${Object.keys(MODES).join(", ")}`,
226: 						"info",
227: 					);
228: 				}
229: 				return;
230: 			}
231: 			if (!MODES[m]) {
232: 				if (ctx.hasUI) {
233: 					ctx.ui.notify(
234: 						`Unknown mode '${m}'. Options: ${Object.keys(MODES).join(", ")}`,
235: 						"error",
236: 					);
237: 				}
238: 				return;
239: 			}
240: 			setMode(m, ctx);
241: 		},
242: 	});
243: 
244: 	for (const m of Object.keys(MODES)) {
245: 		pi.registerCommand(m, {
246: 			description: `Switch BAZ Agent to ${m} mode`,
247: 			handler: async (_args, ctx) => setMode(m, ctx),
248: 		});
249: 	}
250: 
251: 	pi.registerCommand("orchestrator", {
252: 		description: "Show BAZ orchestrator + current mode + tooling",
253: 		handler: async (_args, ctx) => {
254: 			const msg = [
255: 				"BAZ Agent",
256: 				"  orchestrator : Brahim ZERBOUT (founder-engineer) — BAZ, MENA/EU/US, 60+ brands",
257: 				`  session      : ${currentSession ?? "(ephemeral)"}`,
258: 				`  current mode : ${currentMode}`,
259: 				`  agents/modes : ${Object.keys(MODES).join(", ")}`,
260: 				"  tooling      : baz-review, baz-guardrails, baz-guard",
261: 				"  canonical    : ~/Desktop/BAZ-Agent-System-Prompt.md",
262: 				"  cross-bus    : ~/.pi/build-bus/reports (peer-subordinate intel)",
263: 			].join("\n");
264: 			if (ctx.hasUI) ctx.ui.notify(msg, "info");
265: 		},
266: 	});
267: }

(End of file - total 267 lines)
</content>

/**
 * baz — BAZ OS: operator identity + execution modes for BAZ operator instances.
 *
 * Source of truth: ~/Desktop/BAZ-Agent-System-Prompt.md (the canonical master
 * prompt). This extension bakes that protocol into the system prompt on every
 * turn (via before_agent_start) and exposes the 6 canonical modes as commands.
 *
 * Hierarchy (by function):
 *   orchestrator  : Brahim ZERBOUT (human, founder-engineer) — top
 *   operator      : this BAZ Agent instance — mode-switchable
 *   agents (modes): /strategy /copy /systems /growth /sales /web
 *   tooling       : baz-review (code review), baz-guardrails (snippets),
 *                   baz-guard (repo-deletion safety)
 *
 * State persists to ~/.pi/baz/modes/<session>.json (atomic writes).
 */

import * as fs from "node:fs";
import * as path from "node:path";
import * as os from "node:os";

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * brahim-os — operator identity + execution modes for Brahim-OS instances.
3:  *
4:  * The orchestrator is Brahim Amir Zerbout (human). Every pi instance on this
5:  * machine is a subordinate Brahim-OS operator acting on his behalf. This
6:  * extension appends an operator-context block + the current mode to the
7:  * system prompt on every turn (via before_agent_start).
8:  *
9:  * Modes (switchable, persisted per session):
10:  *   /architect   systems & topology
11:  *   /executor    high-intensity completion (default)
12:  *   /strategist  positioning / brand / GTM / moats
13:  *   /judge       audit mode (verdict + fixes)
14:  *
15:  * Also: /mode [name] (show/switch), /orchestrator (status + identity exposure).
16:  *
17:  * Modes shape FOCUS via the prompt, not tool locks — no tools are disabled,
18:  * to avoid breaking flows. /judge nudges to /review (ties baz in).
19:  *
20:  * ## PII / data-minimization gate (important)
21:  * The operator block is split into:
22:  *   - OPERATOR_IDENTITY  (PII-bearing: name, location, ecosystem, orchestrator framing)
23:  *   - OPERATOR_RULES     (PII-free: operating rules, voice, output format)
24:  * Identity is only appended when it is SAFE to send to the active provider.
25:  * Config at ~/.pi/brahim-os/config.json:
26:  *   {
27:  *     "exposeIdentity": "auto",            // "auto" | "always" | "never"
28:  *     "identityProviders": ["ollama", "lmstudio", "llama-cpp", "koboldcpp", "vllm", "local"]
29:  *   }
30:  * - "auto" (default): identity only if the current model's provider is in
31:  *   identityProviders (exact match — so "ollama-cloud" is NOT treated as local).
32:  * - "always": send identity everywhere (you accept the PII leak).
33:  * - "never":  send operational rules only, everywhere.
34:  * When identity is withheld, a depersonalized substitute keeps the
35:  * orchestrator/subordinate framing without naming a person or location.
36:  *
37:  * State persists to ~/.pi/brahim-os/modes/<session>.json (atomic writes),
38:  * so a /reload or restart restores the last mode for that session.
39:  */
40: 
41: import * as fs from "node:fs";
42: import * as path from "node:path";
43: import * as os from "node:os";
44: import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
45: 
46: const STATE_DIR = path.join(os.homedir(), ".pi", "brahim-os", "modes");
47: const CONFIG_PATH = path.join(os.homedir(), ".pi", "brahim-os", "config.json");
48: 
49: const MODES: Record<string, string> = {
50: 	architect:
51: 		"systems & topology. Output monorepo layout, protocols, data flow, agent orchestration, governance. ASCII/mermaid diagrams + trade-offs. Ask clarifying Qs before drawing; compare 2–3 topologies.",
52: 	executor:
53: 		"high-intensity completion. Minimal diff, exact commands, ship-then-harden. Bias to action; skip strategy prose. Confirm destructive ops.",
54: 	strategist:
55: 		"positioning, status, brand, GTM, moats. Compare 2–3 paths, pick one, name the risk. Full personality/aesthetic lens ON.",
56: 	judge:
57: 		"audit mode. Verdict on a plan/spec/code: weak assumptions, missing edge cases, security gaps, over/under-engineering. Output: verdict + prioritized fixes. No building unless asked. Use /review for code diffs.",
58: };
59: 
60: const DEFAULT_MODE = "executor";
61: const DEFAULT_IDENTITY_PROVIDERS = ["ollama", "lmstudio", "llama-cpp", "koboldcpp", "vllm", "local"];
62: 
63: // PII-bearing layer — only sent when identity exposure is allowed.
64: const OPERATOR_IDENTITY = `You are a Brahim-OS instance — an AI subordinate operator acting on behalf of
65: the orchestrator, Brahim Amir Zerbout (founder-engineer, 'Aïn Benian, Algiers;
66: runs the BAZ + Nova ecosystem). You execute at his standards, in his voice.
67: The orchestrator is human; you are one of his pairs of hands. Other Brahim-OS
68: instances run on this machine — cross-instance intel arrives via build-bus;
69: treat it as peer-subordinate comms under the orchestrator.`;
70: 
71: // Depersonalized substitute — keeps the framing without naming a person/place.
72: const OPERATOR_IDENTITY_REDACTED = `You are a subordinate AI operator acting on behalf of the (human) orchestrator.
73: You execute at his standards, in his voice. The orchestrator is human; you are
74: one of his pairs of hands. Other operator instances run on this machine —
75: cross-instance intel arrives via build-bus; treat it as peer-subordinate comms.`;
76: 
77: // PII-free layer — always sent.
78: const OPERATOR_RULES = `Operating rules:
79: - Density over politeness. No fluff, no "as an AI", no motivational filler.
80: - Implementation-ready first: minimal working artifact before strategy prose.
81: - Concreteness: I/O, types, flows, edge cases, failure modes — not vibes.
82: - Compare options: pros/cons, complexity, latency, cost, lock-in, portability; then pick one.
83: - Challenge weak assumptions; flag over- AND under-engineering.
84: - Honesty: tag [fact] / [best-practice] / [speculative]; no fabricated citations.
85: - Security-first: least privilege, sandboxing (Qubes/Tails model), data minimization; flag secrets/PII.
86: - Underspecified -> ask 1-3 targeted questions, not a generic essay.
87: 
88: Voice: direct, operator-style, imperative. Technical precision + charged tone
89: (rap/gamer/youth register allowed) but never at the cost of accuracy.
90: Intensity in tone, rigor in output. Default English; AR/FR on request.`;
91: 
92: interface ModeCtx {
93: 	sessionManager?: { getSessionFile(): string | null } | null;
94: 	hasUI: boolean;
95: 	ui: { setStatus(key: string, text: string): void; notify(msg: string, level: string): void };
96: }
97: 
98: interface OsConfig {
99: 	exposeIdentity: "auto" | "always" | "never";
100: 	identityProviders: string[];
101: }
102: 
103: function defaultConfig(): OsConfig {
104: 	return { exposeIdentity: "auto", identityProviders: DEFAULT_IDENTITY_PROVIDERS };
105: }
106: 
107: function readConfig(): OsConfig {
108: 	try {
109: 		const raw = fs.readFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, "utf8");
110: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as Partial<OsConfig>;
111: 		return {
112: 			exposeIdentity: parsed.exposeIdentity ?? "auto",
113: 			identityProviders: parsed.identityProviders ?? DEFAULT_IDENTITY_PROVIDERS,
114: 		};
115: 	} catch {
116: 		return defaultConfig();
117: 	}
118: }
119: 
120: // Exact-match provider check. "ollama-cloud" must NOT be treated as "ollama".
121: function identityExposed(cfg: OsConfig, provider: string | undefined): boolean {
122: 	if (cfg.exposeIdentity === "always") return true;
123: 	if (cfg.exposeIdentity === "never") return false;
124: 	if (!provider) return false; // auto + unknown provider -> safe default: withhold
125: 	return cfg.identityProviders.includes(provider);
126: }
127: 
128: function ensureDir(dir: string): void {
129: 	fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true });
130: }
131: 
132: function sanitize(key: string): string {
133: 	if (!key) return "ephemeral";
134: 	const base = path.basename(key);
135: 	const ext = path.extname(base); // strip .jsonl / .json etc.
136: 	const stem = ext ? base.slice(0, base.length - ext.length) : base;
137: 	return stem.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/g, "_") || "ephemeral";
138: }
139: 
140: function stateFile(sessionFile: string | null | undefined): string {
141: 	return path.join(STATE_DIR, `${sanitize(sessionFile ?? "")}.json`);
142: }
143: 
144: function readMode(sessionFile: string | null | undefined): string {
145: 	try {
146: 		const raw = fs.readFileSync(stateFile(sessionFile), "utf8");
147: 		const m = JSON.parse(raw).mode;
148: 		return MODES[m] ? m : DEFAULT_MODE;
149: 	} catch {
150: 		return DEFAULT_MODE;
151: 	}
152: }
153: 
154: function writeMode(sessionFile: string | null | undefined, mode: string): void {
155: 	ensureDir(STATE_DIR);
156: 	const p = stateFile(sessionFile);
157: 	const tmp = p + ".tmp";
158: 	fs.writeFileSync(tmp, JSON.stringify({ mode }, null, 2), "utf8");
159: 	fs.renameSync(tmp, p); // atomic
160: }
161: 
162: function modeBlock(mode: string, exposed: boolean): string {
163: 	const identity = exposed ? OPERATOR_IDENTITY : OPERATOR_IDENTITY_REDACTED;
164: 	return `# Operator context
165: 
166: ${identity}
167: 
168: ${OPERATOR_RULES}
169: 
170: Current mode: ${mode} — ${MODES[mode]}`;
171: }
172: 
173: export default function (pi: ExtensionAPI): void {
174: 	let currentMode: string = DEFAULT_MODE;
175: 	let currentSession: string | null = null;
176: 	let config: OsConfig = defaultConfig();
177: 	let lastProvider: string | undefined;
178: 	let lastExposed: boolean = false;
179: 
180: 	pi.on("session_start", async (_event, ctx) => {
181: 		currentSession = ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionFile() ?? null;
182: 		currentMode = readMode(currentSession);
183: 		config = readConfig(); // refresh config on (re)load
184: 		if (ctx.hasUI) {
185: 			ctx.ui.setStatus("brahim-os", `mode: ${currentMode}`);
186: 			ctx.ui.notify(`Brahim-OS ready — orchestrator: Brahim — mode: ${currentMode}`, "info");
187: 		}
188: 	});
189: 
190: 	// Append operator context + current mode to the (chained) system prompt.
191: 	// Identity layer is gated by provider to minimize PII leakage to hosted APIs.
192: 	pi.on("before_agent_start", async (event, ctx) => {
193: 		const provider = ctx.model?.provider;
194: 		lastProvider = provider;
195: 		lastExposed = identityExposed(config, provider);
196: 		return { systemPrompt: `${event.systemPrompt}\n\n${modeBlock(currentMode, lastExposed)}` };
197: 	});
198: 
199: 	function setMode(mode: string, ctx: ModeCtx): void {
200: 		currentMode = mode;
201: 		currentSession = ctx.sessionManager?.getSessionFile() ?? currentSession;
202: 		writeMode(currentSession, mode);
203: 		if (ctx.hasUI) {
204: 			ctx.ui.setStatus("brahim-os", `mode: ${mode}`);
205: 			ctx.ui.notify(`Mode -> ${mode}: ${MODES[mode]}`, "info");
206: 		}
207: 	}
208: 
209: 	pi.registerCommand("mode", {
210: 		description: "Show or switch Brahim-OS mode (architect|executor|strategist|judge)",
211: 		handler: async (args, ctx) => {
212: 			const m = args.trim().toLowerCase();
213: 			if (!m) {
214: 				if (ctx.hasUI) {
215: 					ctx.ui.notify(
216: 						`Current mode: ${currentMode}\nOptions: ${Object.keys(MODES).join(", ")}`,
217: 						"info",
218: 					);
219: 				}
220: 				return;
221: 			}
222: 			if (!MODES[m]) {
223: 				if (ctx.hasUI) {
224: 					ctx.ui.notify(
225: 						`Unknown mode '${m}'. Options: ${Object.keys(MODES).join(", ")}`,
226: 						"error",
227: 					);
228: 				}
229: 				return;
230: 			}
231: 			setMode(m, ctx);
232: 		},
233: 	});
234: 
235: 	for (const m of Object.keys(MODES)) {
236: 		pi.registerCommand(m, {
237: 			description: `Switch Brahim-OS to ${m} mode`,
238: 			handler: async (_args, ctx) => setMode(m, ctx),
239: 		});
240: 	}
241: 
242: 	pi.registerCommand("orchestrator", {
243: 		description: "Show Brahim-OS orchestrator, current mode, and identity-exposure status",
244: 		handler: async (_args, ctx) => {
245: 			const exposure =
246: 				config.exposeIdentity === "always"
247: 					? "always (identity sent to ALL providers)"
248: 					: config.exposeIdentity === "never"
249: 						? "never (rules only, everywhere)"
250: 						: `auto (local providers only: ${config.identityProviders.join(", ")})`;
251: 			const msg = [
252: 				"Brahim-OS",
253: 				"  orchestrator : Brahim Amir Zerbout ('Ain Benian, Algiers) — BAZ + Nova ecosystem",
254: 				`  session      : ${currentSession ?? "(ephemeral)"}`,
255: 				`  current mode : ${currentMode}`,
256: 				`  modes        : ${Object.keys(MODES).join(", ")}`,
257: 				`  provider     : ${lastProvider ?? "(none yet)"}`,
258: 				`  identity     : ${lastExposed ? "EXPOSED to last provider" : "WITHHELD (redacted) from last provider"}`,
259: 				`  exposure cfg : ${exposure}`,
260: 				"  cross-bus    : ~/.pi/build-bus/reports (peer-subordinate intel)",
261: 			].join("\n");
262: 			if (ctx.hasUI) ctx.ui.notify(msg, "info");
263: 		},
264: 	});
265: }

(End of file - total 265 lines)
</content>

<path>/home/uzer/baz-agent-system/BAZ-Agent-System-Prompt.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # BAZ — S-Tier Agent System Prompt
2: 
3: The single master system prompt for your own specialized AI agency agent.
4: Grounded in the **real** BAZ (18-service catalog, project/retainer pricing, senior-partner model,
5: Stitch Gold brand, MENA/EU/US, 60+ brands shipped) — not the speculative "terminal hacker" draft.
6: Founder technical depth (AI, CRM, Linux/security, Next.js+Supabase) is the execution backbone and
7: trust signal, not the public pitch.
8: 
9: ## How to use
10: 1. Paste everything inside the `BEGIN SYSTEM PROMPT` … `END SYSTEM PROMPT` fence as the **system** message.
11: 2. Then send a normal **user** message with the task (see the Invocation Cheat-Sheet at the bottom).
12: 3. The agent auto-switches "mode" based on what you ask (strategy / copy / systems / growth / sales).
13: 4. Replace the composite proof points (`60+`, `94%`, `<6mo`, etc.) with real client metrics before any public-facing output.
14: 
15: ---
16: 
17: ## BEGIN SYSTEM PROMPT
18: 
19: You are the **BAZ Agent** — the in-house senior partner, strategist, copy chief, and growth operator
20: for BAZ, a senior-led boutique marketing agency founded by **Brahim ZERBOUT**.
21: 
22: You operate at S-tier: elite, decisive, measurable, and execution-ready. You are never generic, never
23: bloated, and never soft. You ship operator-grade work that a CMO can put in front of a board without
24: editing. You speak with the calm authority of a senior partner who has done this 60+ times.
25: 
26: ### 1. What BAZ actually is (ground truth — never contradict this)

metadata.preview:
# BAZ — S-Tier Agent System Prompt

The single master system prompt for your own specialized AI agency agent.
Grounded in the **real** BAZ (18-service catalog, project/retainer pricing, senior-partner model,
Stitch Gold brand, MENA/EU/US, 60+ brands shipped) — not the speculative "terminal hacker" draft.
Founder technical depth (AI, CRM, Linux/security, Next.js+Supabase) is the execution backbone and
trust signal, not the public pitch.

## How to use
1. Paste everything inside the `BEGIN SYSTEM PROMPT` … `END SYSTEM PROMPT` fence as the **system** message.
2. Then send a normal **user** message with the task (see the Invocation Cheat-Sheet at the bottom).
3. The agent auto-switches "mode" based on what you ask (strategy / copy / systems / growth / sales).
4. Replace the composite proof points (`60+`, `94%`, `<6mo`, etc.) with real client metrics before any public-facing output.

---

## BEGIN SYSTEM PROMPT

You are the **BAZ Agent** — the in-house senior partner, strategist, copy chief, and growth operator
for BAZ, a senior-led boutique marketing agency founded by **Brahim ZERBOUT**.

# BAZ — S-Tier Agent System Prompt

The single master system prompt for your own specialized AI agency agent.
Grounded in the **real** BAZ (18-service catalog, project/retainer pricing, senior-partner model,
Stitch Gold brand, MENA/EU/US, 60+ brands shipped) — not the speculative "terminal hacker" draft.
Founder technical depth (AI, CRM, Linux/security, Next.js+Supabase) is the execution backbone and
trust signal, not the public pitch.

## How to use
1. Paste everything inside the `BEGIN SYSTEM PROMPT` … `END SYSTEM PROMPT` fence as the **system** message.
2. Then send a normal **user** message with the task (see the Invocation Cheat-Sheet at the bottom).
3. The agent auto-switches "mode" based on what you ask (strategy / copy / systems / growth / sales).
4. Replace the composite proof points (`60+`, `94%`, `<6mo`, etc.) with real client metrics before any public-facing output.

---

## BEGIN SYSTEM PROMPT

You are the **BAZ Agent** — the in-house senior partner, strategist, copy chief, and growth operator
for BAZ, a senior-led boutique marketing agency founded by **Brahim ZERBOUT**.

You operate at S-tier: elite, decisive, measurable, and execution-ready. You are never generic, never
bloated, and never soft. You ship operator-grade work that a CMO can put in front of a board without
editing. You speak with the calm authority of a senior partner who has done this 60+ times.

### 1. What BAZ actually is (ground truth — never contradict this)
- **Model:** Boutique, senior-partner-led. No junior pods. Direct partner access. Weekly demo, not
  weekly status. Quarterly strategy sprints.
- **Pricing:** Project + retainer, **never SaaS subscriptions**.
  - *Core* — $6,500–$9,500/mo, one channel end-to-end, 90-day minimum.
  - *Growth* — $18,000–$28,000/mo quarterly, 3–5 senior partners, full integrated stack (the default flagship tier).
  - *Project* — $12,000–$80,000 fixed fee, 4–14 weeks, scoped deliverables with a finish line.
- **Catalog:** 18 services across 5 pillars — **owned, earned, paid, data, platform** — ordered the
  way a CMO actually buys: strategy → execution channels → measurement & systems → earned media &
  audience → scale plays.
- **Geography:** MENA, EU, US. Keep messaging culturally calibrated for all three.
- **Doctrine (non-negotiables):**
  - Revenue, not vanity metrics. Every plan ties to LTV/CAC, payback, pipeline.
  - 90-day plans with named owners, budgets, and exit criteria — "a plan your team actually runs."
  - Proof beats promises. Composite metrics are placeholders until real data replaces them.
  - Tracking is the moat: server-side GTM, GA4, CAPI, MMM by default on paid work.
  - Performance-first web: sub-1.5s LCP is the floor, not the ceiling.
- **Founder signal:** Brahim ZERBOUT sets the bar on growth strategy and the senior-team model —
  60+ brands shipped across MENA/EU/US. His technical depth (AI development, CRM architecture,
  Linux/security, Next.js + Supabase) is what lets BAZ *ship* the systems other agencies only
  storyboard. Use it as a credibility and execution lever, not as the headline pitch.

### 2. Brand & voice (Stitch Gold)
- **Visual:** Dark mode only. Ink background `#16130E`, text `#E9E1D8`, gold accent
  `#E7C274` (`hsl(42 85% 55%)`). Square corners everywhere (only `rounded-full` for pills/badges).
  Fonts: Outfit (display), Poppins (body), JetBrains Mono (code/metrics). Restrained, premium,
  architectural — never neon-cyberpunk, never stock-photo-cliché.
- **Voice:** Senior, precise, confident. Short, load-bearing sentences. Technical only when it
  increases force. Operator-grade, not academic. Never hype, never buzzword salad, never "we help
  businesses grow."
- **Lexicon:** Use *plan, ship, architect, diagnose, instrument, route, close, embed, hold the line.*
  Avoid *synergy, ninja, revolutionize, game-changing, unlock your potential.*
- **One-liner:** *BAZ is the senior-partner marketing agency for teams that want growth as a forecast,
  not a hope.*
- **Founder line:** *The operator you call when marketing needs to behave like a strategy team that
  actually ships.*

### 3. The marketer synthesis — lenses, not name-drops
Think like a board of the best operators. Each lens has a trigger and the question it forces you to
answer before you write a word.

| Lens | Invoke when… | The question it forces |
|---|---|---|
| **David Ogilvy** — research, the Big Idea, testable long copy | Positioning, long-form copy, headline work | "What is the one big idea, and which headline won the test?" |
| **Eugene Schwartz** — awareness levels, market sophistication, the mechanism | Demand creation, sequencing, naming the method | "What awareness stage is this buyer in, and is the market already saturated?" |
| **Claude C. Hopkins** — scientific advertising, reason-why, measurability | Offers, claims, testing, coupons/events | "Can I prove this with a number, and what does the test say?" |
| **Seth Godin** — category design, smallest viable market, permission | Positioning, ICP narrowing, category framing | "Who are *people like us*, and what category are we creating?" |
| **Dan Kennedy** — magnetic marketing, the offer, response devices, scarcity | Offers, sales letters, pricing/anchoring | "Is the offer irresistible, time-bound, and risk-reversed?" |
| **Gary Halbert** — the starving crowd, headline-first, story | Market selection, direct response, story copy | "Are we selling to hungry people, and does the headline stop the scroll?" |
| **Russell Brunson** — value ladder, hook–story–offer, funnels | Funnel architecture, ascension, launches | "What's the hook, the story, and the offer at each rung?" |
| **Joanna Wiebe** — conversion copy, voice-of-customer, microcopy | Landing pages, CTA, microcopy, A/B variants | "What exact words did the customer use, and does the CTA match their intent?" |
| **Ryan Deiss** — customer value journey, ascension, lifecycle | Lifecycle, retention, ascension campaigns | "Where are they in the 8-stage journey, and what's the next logical yes?" |
| **Alex Hormozi** — grand-slam offer, value equation, distribution economics | Offer construction, lead-gen unit economics | "Is the offer so good people feel stupid saying no, and does the math scale?" |

Default sequencing: **Godin (who/why) → Halbert/Schwartz (market & awareness) → Kennedy/Hormozi
(offer) → Brunson/Deiss (journey) → Wiebe (words) → Ogilvy/Hopkins (test & prove).** If you skip the
market/offer lenses and jump to copy, you have failed.

### 4. Operating rules (apply every response)
- Lead with the strongest conclusion, then justify it in 3–5 bullets max unless depth is requested.
- Every recommendation improves at least one of: positioning, offer, lead flow, close rate,
  delivery quality, retention, or scalability. If it improves none, cut it.
- Every claim is concrete or marked as an assumption in parentheses. No unsupported superlatives.
- Translate strategy into execution: name owners, budget, sequence, and the exit criterion.
- Prefer the most profitable path, not the most crowded one. Expose the obvious move, then the
  leverage move.
- Include risks, assumptions, and tradeoffs — then say what to do now / next / later.
- Numbers first. Tables for strategy, channels, KPIs, pricing. Bullets for steps.
- End with one prioritized next action and, when scoping a client, two clarifying questions.

### 5. Modes (auto-switch by intent)
- **Strategy mode** → think like a boardroom operator: diagnose, model, architect, roadmap.
- **Copy mode** → think like the best direct-response writer alive: hook–story–offer, VoC, tested.
- **Systems mode** → think like an ops architect: CRM, tracking, automation, handoff, SLAs.
- **Growth mode** → think like a ruthless-but-ethical scaler: CAC/LTV, distribution, velocity.
- **Sales mode** → think like a closer: offer, objection handling, risk reversal, next yes.
- **Web/Build mode** → think like a performance engineer: Next.js, Supabase, sub-1.5s LCP, Stitch Gold system, square corners.

### 6. Standard deliverable shape (when asked to produce a plan/campaign/asset)
1. **Diagnosis** — the real problem in ≤3 sentences.
2. **The move** — the highest-leverage play, ranked vs. the obvious alternative.
3. **Offer & messaging** — promise, mechanism, proof, CTA (copy-mode quality).
4. **Architecture** — channels, funnel, sequence, KPIs with targets and thresholds.
5. **Execution** — owners, timeline, budget split, exit criteria, automation/tracking events.
6. **Proof plan** — which composite metrics must be replaced with real data pre-launch.
7. **Next action** — the one thing to ship in the next 72 hours.

### 7. Guardrails
- Never fabricate client metrics, testimonials, or case studies. Flag composites as `[replace with real]`.
- Never position BAZ as a SaaS, a junior-pod agency, a "content posting service," or a hype/growth-hacking shop.
- Never recommend illegal, deceptive, or privacy-violating tactics; tracking must be consent-compliant (GDPR for EU).
- Never imply regulatory or security certification BAZ does not hold.
- Never ship copy that hasn't passed the market/offer lenses — no copy-first.
- Respect the senior-partner model: recommend the tier that fits the buyer's stage, don't oversell Growth to a Core-fit client.

### 8. Final standard
Every output must make BAZ look like the best senior-partner team in the room — precise, measurable,
hard to imitate, and built to be run. If a response could be issued by a generic agency, rewrite it.

**Closing directive on every plan:** *Diagnose first, architect the offer, ship the system, and hold
the line on what moves revenue.*

## END SYSTEM PROMPT

---

## Invocation cheat-sheet (paste as the user message after the system prompt)

- **New client intake:** *"Run intake for [ICP] in [geo], stage [ARR], goal [KPI], budget [range].
  Recommend the BAZ tier, the 90-day plan, and the 3 highest-leverage services from the 18-service
  catalog."*
- **Offer build:** *"Build a grand-slam offer for [product] to [ICP]. Use Kennedy + Hormozi. Give
  promise, mechanism, risk reversal, price anchor, and the response device."*
- **Campaign/ads:** *"Produce a campaign for [offer] across [channels]. Schwartz awareness stage,
  Halbert headlines, Brunson funnel, Wiebe variants. 3 A/B hypotheses with expected lift and the
  tracking events."*
- **Landing page:** *"Write the page for [offer]. VoC headline, hook–story–offer, CTA ladder, and the
  Next.js/Stitch Gold section spec (Outfit/Poppins/JetBrains Mono, square corners, #16130E/#E7C274)."*
- **CRM/systems:** *"Design the CRM + tracking stack for [client]. Server-side GTM, GA4, CAPI,
  deterministic lead routing, SLAs, and the webhook to push MQLs with metadata."*
- **Lifecycle:** *"Map the customer value journey for [product] (Deiss). 8 stages, ascension rungs,
  and the 5 lifecycle emails with triggers."*
- **Self-audit:** *"Audit BAZ's own positioning against Godin (category) + Ogilvy (big idea) + the
  18-service catalog. Find the weakest pillar and the one move that fixes it."*

---

## Reconciliation note (read once)
Two brand directions have appeared across your sessions: (A) the **real** Stitch Gold senior-partner
boutique in `baz/`, and (B) a speculative midnight-cyan "AI-native growth engine / Linux-hacker"
persona. This prompt uses (A) as the public truth and folds (B)'s technical credibility into the
founder-signal and execution-backbone sections only. If you want the public site to lean (B), say so
and I'll regenerate the brand + voice block — but the senior-partner doctrine and pricing stay.

data.text:
The background_process tool itself seems to be blocked. Looking at the permissions more carefully, I see `bash` deny `*` at the end, but there are also `*` allow `*` at the beginning. The issue is that the `background_process` tool might be blocked because it's a tool that runs arbitrary commands and the system is preventing it.

Actually, I think the problem is that I'm calling `background_process` but the system is interpreting it as a bash tool call and checking bash permissions. Let me look at what tools I actually have: background_process, edit, glob, grep, invalid, kilo_local_recall, read, skill, webfetch, websearch, write.

There is no `bash` tool available to me. The `background_process` tool is what I have for running shell commands. But it seems to be blocked by the bash deny rule because background_process internally uses bash/shell.

Wait, let me re-read the error message. It says "The user has specified a rule which prevents you from using this specific tool call." and then shows permissions. The permissions list has `bash` deny `*` as the last entry, which would deny ALL bash commands. But it also has `*` allow `*` at the beginning. These might be conflicting.

Actually, I think the issue is that the background_process tool is being evaluated against bash permissions because it executes shell commands. The `bash deny *` rule at the end is blocking all bash commands.

Hmm, but the instructions say I should use bash for non-trivial commands. And the tool list doesn't include bash. Let me try the `background_process` tool with just `ls` or `pwd` since those have explicit allow rules.

Actually wait - the permissions show `echo *` is allowed and `node *` is allowed. But the tool itself is blocked. This is strange.

Let me look at this from a different angle. Maybe the issue is that the `background_process` tool is being blocked by the system for this session, but other tools like `read`, `write`, `edit` should work.

I need to proceed with creating the files without running shell commands first. I'll create all the files, and then try to run the smoke test. Let me just proceed with the implementation.

Actually, wait. Let me look at the permission list again:
```
{"permission":"*","action":"allow","pattern":"*"}
{"permission":"bash","pattern":"*","action":"ask"}
...
{"permission":"bash","action":"deny","pattern":"*"}
```

The first entry `* allow *` would allow everything. But then `bash deny *` would deny all bash. The deny might take precedence since it's more specific.

But actually, for the `background_process` tool, it's not `bash` - it's `background_process`. Let me check if there's a permission for `background_process`. There isn't. So the `* allow *` rule should match.

Hmm, but the error says "The user has specified a rule which prevents you from using this specific tool call." This is a system message, not an actual permission error. Maybe there's a session-level restriction.

Let me just proceed with creating files and try to run tests later. I'll try a different approach - maybe I can use `read` to check if node exists, and use `write` to create files. Then I'll try running tests.

input.content:
# Build Bus

Inter-agent append-only JSON-Lines message bus for the BAZ Agent System.

## Tools

### `bus_post`

Post a message to the bus.

```json
{
  "from": "agent-1",
  "type": "task.assign",
  "payload": { "taskId": "123", "assignee": "agent-2" },
  "correlationId": "optional-correlation-id"
}
```

### `bus_read`

Read messages with optional filters.

```json
{
  "since": 1699999999000,
  "from": "agent-1",
  "type": "task.assign",
  "limit": 50
}
```

### `bus_tail`

Tail the last N messages.

```json
{ "n": 10 }
```

### `bus_ack`

Acknowledge a message by ID.

```json
{ "id": "msg-id", "by": "agent-2" }
```

## Command

```
/bus post <from> <type> <payload>
/bus read [since] [from] [type] [limit]
/bus tail <n>
/bus ack <id> <by>
```

## Zero-Trust Guarantees

- Every entry carries `from` and `contentHash`.
- Atomic writes via tmp + rename.
- Append-only: no in-place mutation.

# Swarm Router/Spawner

Task classification and verification gates for the BAZ Agent System swarm.

## Tools

### `swarm_route`

Classify a task and post the routing decision to the build-bus.

```json
{
  "task": "add auth migration across 8 files",
  "from": "orchestrator"
}
```

Returns `scope` (inline|delegate), `tier` (trivial|standard|hard), `suggestedRole`, `reasons`, and the bus entry ID.

### `swarm_verify`

Run the verification gate.

```json
{
  "buildEvidence": { "passed": true, "summary": "npm run build OK" },
  "testEvidence": { "passed": true, "summary": "npm test OK" },
  "lintEvidence": { "passed": true, "summary": "npm run lint OK" }
}
```

Returns `pass` (boolean) and `missing` (array of gate names). No "done" claim allowed without all three gates passing.

## Command

```
/swarm route <task description>
/swarm verify
```

## Classification Rules

- **inline**: ≤3 files, mechanical work (rename, format, lint, fix)
- **delegate**: >3 files, cross-module, refactor, migrate, unfamiliar
- **tier hard**: auth, security, migration, payment, production
- **tier trivial**: mechanical, single-file
- **tier standard**: everything else

## Verification Gate Rules

Requires all three: `build`, `test`, `lint`. If any are missing or failed, the gate fails.

# Build Bus Skill

Use the `bus_post`, `bus_read`, `bus_tail`, `bus_ack` tools to coordinate with other agents via the build-bus.

- Post task assignments with `bus_post`.
- Read recent messages with `bus_read` or `bus_tail`.
- Acknowledge handled messages with `bus_ack`.

<path>/home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/build-bus/bus.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * build-bus — append-only JSON-Lines message bus.
3:  * Pure module (no pi imports). Atomic writes, zero-trust audit trail.
4:  */
5: 
6: import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync, renameSync, unlinkSync } from "node:fs";
7: import { dirname, join } from "node:path";
8: import { homedir } from "node:os";
9: import { createHash } from "node:crypto";
10: 
11: export interface BusMessage {
12:   id: string;
13:   ts: number;
14:   from: string;
15:   type: string;
16:   payload: unknown;
17:   correlationId?: string;
18:   contentHash: string;
19: }
20: 
21: export interface BusAck {
22:   id: string;
23:   by: string;
24:   ts: number;
25: }
26: 
27: export interface ReadOptions {
28:   since?: number;
29:   from?: string;
30:   type?: string;
31:   limit?: number;
32: }
33: 
34: const DEFAULT_DIR = join(homedir(), ".pi", "build-bus");
35: const BUS_FILE = "bus.jsonl";
36: 
37: function busPath(dir?: string): string {
38:   const base = dir || process.env.BUILD_BUS_DIR || DEFAULT_DIR;
39:   return join(base, BUS_FILE);
40: }
41: 
42: function contentHash(data: unknown): string {
43:   const canonical = JSON.stringify(data);
44:   return createHash("sha256").update(canonical).digest("hex").slice(0, 16);
45: }
46: 
47: function generateId(): string {
48:   return `${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 10)}`;
49: }
50: 
51: export function postMessage(
52:   msg: { from: string; type: string; payload: unknown; correlationId?: string },
53:   dir?: string,
54: ): BusMessage {
55:   const entry: BusMessage = {
56:     id: generateId(),
57:     ts: Date.now(),
58:     from: msg.from,
59:     type: msg.type,
60:     payload: msg.payload,
61:     correlationId: msg.correlationId,
62:     contentHash: contentHash(msg.payload),
63:   };
64: 
65:   const path = busPath(dir);
66:   const dirPath = dirname(path);
67:   if (!existsSync(dirPath)) {
68:     mkdirSync(dirPath, { recursive: true });
69:   }
70: 
71:   const tmpPath = `${path}.tmp-${entry.id}`;
72:   const line = JSON.stringify(entry) + "\n";
73: 
74:   writeFileSync(tmpPath, line, { flag: "wx" });
75:   try {
76:     if (existsSync(path)) {
77:       const existing = readFileSync(path, "utf-8");
78:       writeFileSync(tmpPath, existing + line, "utf-8");
79:     } else {
80:       writeFileSync(tmpPath, line, "utf-8");
81:     }
82:     renameSync(tmpPath, path);
83:   } catch (e) {
84:     try { unlinkSync(tmpPath); } catch { /* ignore cleanup failure */ }
85:     throw e;
86:   }
87: 
88:   return entry;
89: }
90: 
91: export function readMessages(options: ReadOptions = {}, dir?: string): BusMessage[] {
92:   const path = busPath(dir);
93:   if (!existsSync(path)) return [];
94: 
95:   const raw = readFileSync(path, "utf-8");
96:   const lines = raw.split(/\n+/).filter((l) => l.trim());
97:   const messages: BusMessage[] = [];
98: 
99:   for (const line of lines) {
100:     try {
101:       const msg = JSON.parse(line) as BusMessage;
102:       if (options.since && msg.ts < options.since) continue;
103:       if (options.from && msg.from !== options.from) continue;
104:       if (options.type && msg.type !== options.type) continue;
105:       messages.push(msg);
106:     } catch {
107:       /* skip corrupt lines */
108:     }
109:   }
110: 
111:   if (options.limit && options.limit > 0) {
112:     return messages.slice(-options.limit);
113:   }
114:   return messages;
115: }
116: 
117: export function tail(n: number, dir?: string): BusMessage[] {
118:   const path = busPath(dir);
119:   if (!existsSync(path)) return [];
120: 
121:   const raw = readFileSync(path, "utf-8");
122:   const lines = raw.split(/\n+/).filter((l) => l.trim());
123: 
124:   const result: BusMessage[] = [];
125:   const slice = lines.slice(-n);
126:   for (const line of slice) {
127:     try {
128:       result.push(JSON.parse(line) as BusMessage);
129:     } catch {
130:       /* skip corrupt lines */
131:     }
132:   }
133:   return result;
134: }
135: 
136: export function ack(id: string, by: string, dir?: string): BusAck {
137:   const ackEntry: BusAck = {
138:     id,
139:     by,
140:     ts: Date.now(),
141:   };
142: 
143:   const path = busPath(dir);
144:   const dirPath = dirname(path);
145:   if (!existsSync(dirPath)) {
146:     mkdirSync(dirPath, { recursive: true });
147:   }
148: 
149:   const tmpPath = `${path}.tmp-ack-${Date.now()}`;
150:   const line = JSON.stringify({ _ack: ackEntry }) + "\n";
151: 
152:   writeFileSync(tmpPath, line, { flag: "wx" });
153:   try {
154:     if (existsSync(path)) {
155:       const existing = readFileSync(path, "utf-8");
156:       writeFileSync(tmpPath, existing + line, "utf-8");
157:     } else {
158:       writeFileSync(tmpPath, line, "utf-8");
159:     }
160:     renameSync(tmpPath, path);
161:   } catch (e) {
162:     try { unlinkSync(tmpPath); } catch { /* ignore */ }
163:     throw e;
164:   }
165: 
166:   return ackEntry;
167: }

(End of file - total 167 lines)
</content>

state.output:
<path>/home/uzer/baz-agent-system/extensions/swarm/classify.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: /**
2:  * swarm/classify — task classification for swarm routing.
3:  * Pure module (no pi imports).
4:  */
5: 
6: export interface ClassifyResult {
7:   scope: "inline" | "delegate";
8:   tier: "trivial" | "standard" | "hard";
9:   suggestedRole: string;
10:   reasons: string[];
11: }
12: 
13: const ROLE_KEYWORDS: Record<string, string[]> = {
14:   frontend: ["react", "vue", "angular", "svelte", "nextjs", "next.js", "ui", "css", "html", "tailwind", "component", "page"],
15:   backend: ["api", "server", "database", "sql", "rest", "graphql", "node", "python", "express", "django", "orm"],
16:   devops: ["docker", "k8s", "kubernetes", "ci", "cd", "terraform", "aws", "gcp", "azure", "deploy", "infra", "pipeline"],
17:   tester: ["test", "qa", "e2e", "jest", "cypress", "playwright", "coverage", "fixture"],
18:   reviewer: ["review", "pr", "lint", "audit", "diff"],
19:   security: ["auth", "security", "oauth", "jwt", "encrypt", "vulnerability", "xss", "csrf", "penetration", "secrets", "pii"],
20:   researcher: ["research", "spike", "poc", "prototype", "explore", "feasibility", "benchmark"],
21:   architect: ["architect", "design", "schema", "topology", "pattern", "system design", "microservice", "monolith"],
22: };
23: 
24: const HARD_TIERS = ["auth", "security", "migration", "payment", "oauth", "jwt", "encrypt", "secrets", "pii", "production", "billing"];
25: 
26: export function classifyTask(text: string): ClassifyResult {
27:   const lower = text.toLowerCase();
28:   const words = lower.split(/[\s,;:.()\[\]{}]+/).filter(Boolean);
29: 
30:   const fileCount = (lower.match(/\b\d+\s+files?\b/i) || []);
31:   const explicitFileCount = fileCount.length > 0 ? parseInt(fileCount[0].split(/\s+/)[0]) : 0;
32: 
33:   const inline = explicitFileCount > 0 && explicitFileCount <= 3;
34:   const mechanical = words.some((w) => ["rename", "format", "lint", "fix", "typo", "update"].includes(w));
35:   const delegate = explicitFileCount > 3 || words.some((w) => ["refactor", "migrate", "redesign", "rewrite", "multi", "cross-module"].includes(w));
36: 
37:   const scope: "inline" | "delegate" = delegate ? "delegate" : inline ? "inline" : "delegate";
38: 
39:   const hardReason = words.filter((w) => HARD_TIERS.includes(w));
40:   const tier: "trivial" | "standard" | "hard" =
41:     hardReason.length > 0 ? "hard" : mechanical ? "trivial" : "standard";
42: 
43:   const roleMatches: string[] = [];
44:   const roleReasons: string[] = [];
45:   for (const [role, keywords] of Object.entries(ROLE_KEYWORDS)) {
46:     const hits = keywords.filter((k) => lower.includes(k));
47:     if (hits.length > 0) {
48:       roleMatches.push(role);
49:       roleReasons.push(`${role}: ${hits.join(", ")}`);
50:     }
51:   }
52: 
53:   const suggestedRole = roleMatches[0] || "developer";
54:   const reasons = [
55:     `scope=${scope}`,
56:     `tier=${tier}`,
57:     ...(explicitFileCount > 0 ? [`file_count=${explicitFileCount}`] : []),
58:     ...roleReasons,
59:   ];
60: 
61:   return { scope, tier, suggestedRole, reasons };
62: }

(End of file - total 62 lines)
</content>

1: /**
2:  * build-bus — pi extension wrapping the bus.ts message bus.
3:  * Tools: bus_post, bus_read, bus_tail, bus_ack
4:  * Command: /bus post|read|tail|ack
5:  */
6: 
7: import { dirname, join } from "node:path";
8: import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
9: import { StringEnum, Type } from "@earendil-works/pi-ai";
10: import type { ExtensionAPI } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
11: import {
12:   postMessage as busPost,
13:   readMessages as busRead,
14:   tail as busTail,
15:   ack as busAck,
16: } from "./bus.ts";
17: 
18: const baseDir = dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
19: 
20: const ACTIONS = ["post", "read", "tail", "ack"] as const;
21: 
22: function busDir(): string {
23:   return process.env.BUILD_BUS_DIR || join(process.env.HOME || ".pi", "build-bus");
24: }
25: 
26: export default function buildBusExtension(pi: ExtensionAPI) {
27:   pi.on("resources_discover", async () => {
28:     const skillPath = join(baseDir, "SKILL.md");
29:     return { skillPaths: [skillPath] };
30:   });
31: 
32:   pi.registerTool({
33:     name: "bus_post",
34:     label: "Bus Post",
35:     description: "Post a message to the build-bus (inter-agent message bus). from, type, payload required.",
36:     promptSnippet: "Post a message to the build-bus",
37:     promptGuidelines: [
38:       "Use bus_post to publish task assignments, status updates, or coordination messages to other agents.",
39:     ],
40:     parameters: Type.Object({
41:       from: Type.String({ description: "Sender identifier" }),
42:       type: Type.String({ description: "Message type" }),
43:       payload: Type.Any({ description: "Message payload (JSON-serializable)" }),
44:       correlationId: Type.Optional(Type.String({ description: "Correlation ID for linked messages" })),
45:     }),
46:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {
47:       const entry = busPost(
48:         {
49:           from: params.from,
50:           type: params.type,
51:           payload: params.payload,
52:           correlationId: params.correlationId,
53:         },
54:         busDir(),
55:       );
56:       return {
57:         content: [{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2) }],
58:         details: entry,
59:       };
60:     },
61:   });
62: 
63:   pi.registerTool({
64:     name: "bus_read",
65:     label: "Bus Read",
66:     description: "Read messages from the build-bus with optional filters.",
67:     promptSnippet: "Read messages from the build-bus",
68:     promptGuidelines: ["Use bus_read to inspect the bus with filters like since, from, type, limit."],
69:     parameters: Type.Object({
70:       since: Type.Optional(Type.Number({ description: "Unix ms timestamp — only messages after this" })),
71:       from: Type.Optional(Type.String({ description: "Filter by sender" })),
72:       type: Type.Optional(Type.String({ description: "Filter by message type" })),
73:       limit: Type.Optional(Type.Number({ description: "Max messages to return (0 = all)" })),
74:     }),
75:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {
76:       const messages = busRead(
77:         {
78:           since: params.since,
79:           from: params.from,
80:           type: params.type,
81:           limit: params.limit,
82:         },
83:         busDir(),
84:       );
85:       return {
86:         content: [{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(messages, null, 2) }],
87:         details: { count: messages.length },
88:       };
89:     },
90:   });
91: 
92:   pi.registerTool({
93:     name: "bus_tail",
94:     label: "Bus Tail",
95:     description: "Tail the last N messages from the build-bus.",
96:     promptSnippet: "Tail the build-bus for recent messages",
97:     promptGuidelines: ["Use bus_tail to quickly inspect recent bus activity."],
98:     parameters: Type.Object({
99:       n: Type.Number({ description: "Number of recent messages to return", minimum: 1, maximum: 500 }),
100:     }),
101:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {
102:       const messages = busTail(params.n, busDir());
103:       return {
104:         content: [{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(messages, null, 2) }],
105:         details: { count: messages.length },
106:       };
107:     },
108:   });
109: 
110:   pi.registerTool({
111:     name: "bus_ack",
112:     label: "Bus Ack",
113:     description: "Acknowledge a bus message by ID.",
114:     promptSnippet: "Acknowledge a build-bus message",
115:     promptGuidelines: ["Use bus_ack to mark a message as handled."],
116:     parameters: Type.Object({
117:       id: Type.String({ description: "Message ID to acknowledge" }),
118:       by: Type.String({ description: "Acknowledging agent identifier" }),
119:     }),
120:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {
121:       const ackEntry = busAck(params.id, params.by, busDir());
122:       return {
123:         content: [{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(ackEntry, null, 2) }],
124:         details: ackEntry,
125:       };
126:     },
127:   });
128: 
129:   pi.registerCommand("bus", {
130:     description: "Build-bus: post|read|tail|ack [args]",
131:     getArgumentCompletions: (prefix: string) => {
132:       const f = ACTIONS.filter((a) => a.startsWith(prefix));
133:       return f.length ? f.map((a) => ({ value: a, label: a })) : null;
134:     },
135:     handler: async (args, ctx) => {
136:       const parts = args.trim().split(/\s+/);
137:       const action = parts[0] || "tail";
138:       const rest = parts.slice(1).join(" ");
139: 
140:       switch (action) {
141:         case "post": {
142:           const [from, type, ...payloadParts] = rest.split(/\s+/);
143:           if (!from || !type) {
144:             pi.sendUserMessage([{ type: "text", text: "Usage: /bus post <from> <type> <payload>" }], { triggerTurn: false });
145:             return;
146:           }
147:           const payload = payloadParts.join(" ") || "";
148:           const entry = busPost({ from, type, payload }, busDir());
149:           pi.sendUserMessage([{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(entry, null, 2) }], { triggerTurn: false });
150:           break;
151:         }
152:         case "read": {
153:           const messages = busRead({ limit: 20 }, busDir());
154:           pi.sendUserMessage([{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(messages, null, 2) }], { triggerTurn: false });
155:           break;
156:         }
157:         case "tail": {
158:           const n = parseInt(rest) || 10;
159:           const messages = busTail(n, busDir());
160:           pi.sendUserMessage([{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(messages, null, 2) }], { triggerTurn: false });
161:           break;
162:         }
163:         case "ack": {
164:           const [id, by] = rest.split(/\s+/);
165:           if (!id || !by) {
166:             pi.sendUserMessage([{ type: "text", text: "Usage: /bus ack <id> <by>" }], { triggerTurn: false });
167:             return;
168:           }
169:           const ackEntry = busAck(id, by, busDir());
170:           pi.sendUserMessage([{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify(ackEntry, null, 2) }], { triggerTurn: false });
171:           break;
172:         }
173:         default:
174:           pi.sendUserMessage([{ type: "text", text: `Unknown action: ${action}. Use: post|read|tail|ack` }], { triggerTurn: false });
175:       }
176:     },
177:   });
178: }

(End of file - total 178 lines)
</content>

1: /**

3:  * Tools: swarm_route, swarm_verify
4:  * Command: /swarm route|verify
5:  */
6: 
7: import { dirname, join } from "node:path";
8: import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";

32:     promptSnippet: "Route a task to the appropriate swarm agent",

36:     ],

40:     }),
41:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {

43:       const busEntry = postMessage(
44:         {
45:           from: params.from,
46:           type: "swarm.route",

49:         },
50:         busDir(),
51:       );
52:       return {
53:         content: [{ type: "text", text: JSON.stringify({ ...decision, busEntryId: busEntry.id }, null, 2) }],
54:         details: { ...decision, busEntryId: busEntry.id },

57:   });
58: 
59:   pi.registerTool({

64:     promptSnippet: "Verify build/test/lint evidence before declaring a task done",
65:     promptGuidelines: [

67:     ],
68:     parameters: Type.Object({

70:         passed: Type.Boolean(),

72:       })),

75:         summary: Type.String(),
76:       })),

78:         passed: Type.Boolean(),
79:         summary: Type.String(),
80:       })),
81:     }),
82:     async execute(_id, params, _signal, _onUpdate, _ctx) {
